name: Santa Ana Reservoir Loop from Alfarràs
name_original: Ida y vuelta al Embalse de Santa Anna - desde Alfarràs
description: This easy 15.8-mile (25.4 km) road cycling loop from Alfarràs takes you to the Santa Ana Reservoir in about 1 hour and 18 minutes.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Almenar traverse the plains of Lleida, characterized by expansive agricultural areas, fruit orchards, and cereal fields. The region, situated at an elevation of 329 meters, features quiet roads suitable for steady rides and long-distance training. Almenar itself is built on a hill, providing varied terrain within the broader flat landscape of the Segrià comarca. Best road cycling routes around Almenar The most popular road cycling route is Santa Ana Reservoir Loop from Alfarràs, a 15.8 miles (25.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 18 minutes to complete. This easy route offers views of the Santa Ana Reservoir. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Alguaire – Path loop from Alguaire, a moderate 35.7 miles (57.4 km) path. This route winds through agricultural plains and offers a steady ride. Local road cyclists also love the Old Bridge of Alfarràs – Congost de Camporrells loop from Alguaire, a 73.2 miles (117.8 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 5 hours 28 minutes. Road cycling around Almenar is defined by agricultural plains, fruit orchards, and quiet roads. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Almenar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 6 reviews. More than 1000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Almenar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Almenar?
A: There are over 50 road cycling routes around Almenar, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include 15 easy routes, 22 moderate routes, and 13 challenging options, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Almenar?
A: Road cycling routes around Almenar primarily traverse the expansive Lleida Plain, characterized by agricultural areas, fruit orchards, and cereal fields. While the region is generally flat, Almenar itself is built on a hill, introducing some varied terrain. For more challenging rides with significant elevation, the wider Lleida province offers access to the Pyrenees and Montsec mountain ranges.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners or leisurely rides?
A: Yes, Almenar offers several easy routes perfect for beginners or those seeking a relaxed ride. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e942419548/santa-ana-reservoir-loop-from-alfarras" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Ana Reservoir Loop from Alfarràs&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 25.4 km trail that provides pleasant views. Another accessible route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33695685" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alguaire – Church of Benavent de Segrià loop from Almenar&lt;/a&gt;, covering 45 km with gentle elevation.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, the region offers routes with more significant distances and elevation. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31443343" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Bridge of Alfarràs – Congost de Camporrells loop from Alguaire&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 117.8 km trail with over 1200 meters of elevation gain, typically taking around 5 hours 28 minutes to complete.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer scenic views and pass by interesting landmarks. You can enjoy views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1132485" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Ana Reservoir and Dam&lt;/a&gt; on some routes. The wider region also features the Aiguabarreig Segre-Noguera Pallaresa Natural Park and Lo Clot de la Unilla, offering lush vegetation and unique ecosystems. Culturally, you might pass through medieval villages or near the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3082600" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Benavent de Segrià&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any loop trails for road cycling around Almenar?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Almenar are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e942419548/santa-ana-reservoir-loop-from-alfarras" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Ana Reservoir Loop from Alfarràs&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20179029" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alguaire – Path loop from Alguaire&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33695685" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alguaire – Church of Benavent de Segrià loop from Almenar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Almenar?
A: The road cycling routes around Almenar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the quiet roads, the scenic agricultural plains with fruit orchards and cereal fields, and the variety of routes available for different fitness levels.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Almenar?
A: The region generally offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly pleasant, with milder temperatures ideal for longer rides. Summers can be warm, especially in the plains, so early morning or late afternoon rides are often preferred during these months.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, for those looking for longer rides, Almenar and its surroundings offer several options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31443343" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Bridge of Alfarràs – Congost de Camporrells loop from Alguaire&lt;/a&gt; is a significant 117.8 km route. The expansive Lleida Plain also allows for combining routes to create extended journeys through its quiet, well-surfaced roads.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through towns or villages with amenities like cafes?
A: Many routes in the Almenar area will pass through or near small towns and villages, offering opportunities for cafe stops and other amenities. The region's cultural richness means you'll often find local establishments in these settlements, perfect for a break during your ride.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots recommended for road cyclists?
A: While the plains offer wide, open vistas of agricultural landscapes, routes that incorporate the Santa Ana Reservoir provide beautiful water views. For more dramatic scenic viewpoints, the nearby Montsec mountain range, accessible from the wider Lleida province, offers spectacular landscapes and panoramic views, though these routes will be significantly more challenging.

Q: What are the typical road conditions like in the Almenar area?
A: The roads in the Almenar area, particularly within the Lleida Plain, are generally quiet and well-surfaced. They are suitable for endurance rides and touring, providing a smooth experience through the agricultural heartland.
tips: On the border between Catalonia and the Strip, an area with long straights and strong insolation in the summer, always carry water before crossing this area.
View over the Santa Ana reservoir. A good vantage point on one side of the Santa Ana dam and on the other the Alfarrås plain.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of road cycling the Santa Ana Reservoir Loop from Alfarràs?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as easy, making it suitable for most fitness levels. With a distance of approximately 25.4 km and an elevation gain of around 207 meters, it offers a pleasant ride without significant challenges.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: As a racebike route, you can expect primarily paved surfaces, ideal for road cycling. The elevation changes are gradual, contributing to its easy difficulty rating.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or scenic viewpoints along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route features a couple of interesting points. You'll pass by the historic Old Bridge of Alfarràs and enjoy views of the Santa Ana Reservoir and Dam, offering picturesque spots during your ride.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Santa Ana Reservoir Loop from Alfarràs?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, Alfarràs, as the starting point, typically offers public parking options within the town. It's advisable to look for street parking or designated public lots near the route's beginning.

faqs.question: Is the Santa Ana Reservoir Loop from Alfarràs suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Given its 'easy' difficulty grade, moderate distance, and relatively low elevation gain, this loop is an excellent choice for beginner road cyclists looking for an enjoyable and manageable ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route generally depends on local climate. Spring and autumn often provide the most comfortable temperatures for cycling in this region, avoiding the extreme heat of summer or potential chill of winter.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Santa Ana Reservoir Loop from Alfarràs?
faqs.answer: As a road cycling route, dogs are generally not permitted to accompany cyclists on the paved sections for safety reasons. If you plan to bring a dog, consider alternative walking trails or ensure they are safely secured in a bike trailer designed for pets.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Santa Ana Reservoir Loop from Alfarràs. It is a public route accessible to all.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with any other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with sections of several other trails, including El Camí IT2TR1 El Camí de Ponent: de Balaguer a Montserrat, La Litera. BTT. Ruta 2. Yesos y Embalse de Santa Ana, and Camino de Santiago por Huesca.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments near Alfarràs or along the route?
faqs.answer: Alfarràs, as the starting and ending point, will have local cafes and shops where you can find refreshments before or after your ride. Along the loop itself, amenities may be limited, so it's wise to carry sufficient water and snacks.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a road cycling trip on this loop?
faqs.answer: For this easy road cycling loop, we recommend bringing your racebike, a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, water, snacks, a basic repair kit (spare tube, pump), and a phone for navigation or emergencies. Sun protection is also advisable.
